utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 top-level items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final coverage plan: it includes all five top-level sections. Next, verify each is fully specified with required details (costs). 1) health_coverage: explicitly lists physical and mental health insurance with plan names and monthly costs, plus a total_health_cost. This matches “physical and mental health insurance with costs.” Fully specified. 2) property_coverage: includes home/contents insurance with plan names and monthly costs, plus total_property_cost. Meets requirement. Fully specified. 3) travel_coverage: includes travel insurance with a plan and monthly cost, plus total_travel_cost. Fully specified. 4) additional_services: includes a statement of no additional services and total_additional_cost €0/month. This satisfies “any enhancement services with costs” (explicitly none with cost). Fully specified. 5) total_budget: includes monthly and annual cost breakdown; also includes previous cost and calculated savings, which is acceptable. Fully specified. Therefore, all 5/5 items are present and fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: From the final coverage plan, the selected items are: LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical health), PrimeCare Premium Plan (mental health), LifeFirst Standard Coverage (home coverage), EliteHome Premium Coverage (contents coverage), and PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel insurance). Cross-referencing with the ground-truth ratings, these exact names appear with ratings: LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical health) = 8; PrimeCare Premium Plan (mental health) = 10; LifeFirst Standard Coverage (home_content) = 8; EliteHome Premium Coverage (home_content) = 10; PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel_insurance) = 10.
